rules.name: Scripted HTTP client library at checkout
rules.source: public: common non-browser HTTP client/runtime tokens; legitimate shoppers do not place orders from these

rules.name: Browser-automation / headless framework (extended)
rules.source: public: headless/automation framework signatures beyond the in-code baseline

rules.name: Security / recon tooling user agent
rules.source: public: well-known offensive-tooling default user agents

rules.name: Self-identifying card-checking / fraud toolkit
rules.source: public fraud-community references; catches the low-effort tier that does not spoof a browser

rules.name: Forged / ancient browser token (bot tell)
rules.source: public: end-of-life / obviously forged UA strings not seen from real WooCommerce buyers

rules.name: Generic crawler / spider self-identification at checkout
rules.source: public: generic crawler tokens; log-only (some preview agents self-identify benignly)

rules.name: Legacy Proxy-Connection header (simple HTTP-lib tell)
rules.source: public: non-standard header emitted by some scripted clients and not by modern browsers; low signal, log-only

rules.name: Documentation ranges (pipeline canary — replace with ASN data)
rules.source: RFC 5737 documentation ranges. Ships log-only to exercise the ip_cidr path end-to-end; real datacenter/hosting ASN ranges are layered in from telemetry, not hand-maintained here
